# Ozgnos

> Ozgnos is a free, beginner-focused crypto education site. Plain-English lessons on how cryptocurrency, blockchains, wallets, exchanges, DeFi and market data actually work — written to teach, not to sell. Educational only, never financial advice. Funded solely by clearly-disclosed referral links (see https://ozgnos.com/disclosure).

## Library (all lessons, free)

- [Coins, Tokens, and Bitcoin: What's the Difference?](https://ozgnos.com/library/coins-tokens-bitcoin): A coin has its own blockchain, a token is built on top of someone else's, and Bitcoin is just the first and biggest coin.
- [Common Crypto Scams, and How to Spot Them](https://ozgnos.com/library/common-crypto-scams): Almost every crypto scam works by creating urgency, faking authority, or promising free money. Learn the patterns once, and you'll see them coming.
- [Crypto & Taxes: The Basics Nobody Tells Beginners](https://ozgnos.com/library/crypto-and-taxes): Crypto is taxed in most countries, exchanges report to authorities, and crypto-to-crypto swaps often count. The general patterns, the record-keeping habit, and why 'anonymous' is a myth.
- [Crypto Regulation in Plain English (Including MiCA)](https://ozgnos.com/library/crypto-regulation): What crypto regulation actually does for you, how approaches differ around the world, and what the EU's MiCA framework means in practice — without the legal jargon.
- [The Fear & Greed Index: Reading the Market's Mood](https://ozgnos.com/library/fear-and-greed-index): The Fear & Greed Index boils the market's overall emotion down to a single number from 0 to 100 — a snapshot of mood, not a prediction of what comes next.
- [Hot Wallets vs Cold Wallets: Where Should Your Crypto Live?](https://ozgnos.com/library/hot-and-cold-wallets): Hot wallets are connected to the internet, cold wallets aren't — and that one difference decides how safe your crypto is. How to choose, and when it starts to matter.
- [How to Buy Your First Crypto (Step by Step)](https://ozgnos.com/library/how-to-buy-your-first-crypto): A calm, step-by-step walkthrough of your first crypto purchase — choosing an exchange, securing your account, and deciding where your coins live. We won't tell you what to buy.
- [How to Read Crypto Market Data](https://ozgnos.com/library/how-to-read-market-data): Price, market cap, and volume each tell you something useful, but only when you read them together — and knowing what they don't say is just as important.
- [How to Research a Crypto Project (The DYOR Checklist)](https://ozgnos.com/library/how-to-research-a-project): DYOR is advice everyone gives and nobody explains. Here's the actual method: six checks, in order, that turn 'do your own research' into an hour of work anyone can do.
- [Market Cap, Supply & Tokenomics: Why "Cheap" Coins Aren't Cheap](https://ozgnos.com/library/market-cap-and-tokenomics): A $0.001 coin isn't cheap and a $100,000 coin isn't expensive. Market cap, circulating supply, FDV, and token unlocks — the numbers that actually tell you something.
- [Bull Markets, Bear Markets & the Halving: Crypto's Cycles](https://ozgnos.com/library/market-cycles): Crypto moves in violent waves — euphoric bull markets, brutal bear markets, and a famous four-year rhythm. What's real about cycles, what's folklore, and why volatility is the price of admission.
- [Mining & Staking: Where Do Coins Come From?](https://ozgnos.com/library/mining-and-staking): Thousands of computers maintain the blockchain — because they're paid in new coins. Proof of work, proof of stake, and what 'mining' and 'staking' actually mean.
- [Networks and "Layers": Why So Many Chains?](https://ozgnos.com/library/networks-and-layers): There isn't one blockchain — there are many — and "Layer 2" just means a faster, cheaper network built on top of a slower, more secure one.
- [Your Seed Phrase: The One Rule That Matters Most](https://ozgnos.com/library/seed-phrase): Your seed phrase is the master key to everything you own in crypto. Write it down, keep it offline, and never share it with anyone.
- [Stablecoins: The "Digital Dollar," and Why It's Not Risk-Free](https://ozgnos.com/library/stablecoins-explained): A stablecoin is crypto designed to hold a steady value — usually one US dollar — but the calm name hides some real risks worth knowing.
- [Staying Safe: A Crypto Security Checklist](https://ozgnos.com/library/staying-safe-checklist): Good crypto security isn't about being paranoid — it's about a few simple habits that, done consistently, stop almost every common attack.
- [Transactions, Fees, and "Gas"](https://ozgnos.com/library/transactions-fees-gas): Every time you move crypto on a blockchain, you pay a small fee called "gas," and it exists to pay the network and stop people from spamming it.
- [What Are Indicators? Reading the Ozgnos Dashboard](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-are-indicators): Indicators are math applied to past price data to describe what's happening — like a weather forecast for the market: useful for context, never a command to act.
- [What Are NFTs? What You Actually Own, Explained](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-are-nfts): An NFT is a token where every unit is unique — a blockchain record pointing at something. What you actually own when you buy one, what survived the 2021 mania, and what didn't.
- [What Is a Bridge? (And Why the Biggest Hacks Happen There)](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-a-bridge): Blockchains can't talk to each other, so bridges move value between them — by locking coins on one side and minting IOUs on the other. Useful, and the site of crypto's biggest thefts.
- [What Is a Crypto Wallet?](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-a-wallet): A crypto wallet doesn't actually hold your coins — it holds the keys that prove the coins are yours.
- [What Is a Crypto Exchange?](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-an-exchange): An exchange is where you swap regular money for crypto (and back), and there are two kinds — one run by a company, one run by code.
- [What Is Blockchain? The Shared Record Behind It All](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-blockchain): A blockchain is a record book that thousands of computers keep at the same time, so no single one can secretly change it.
- [What Is Cryptocurrency? Start Here.](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-cryptocurrency): Cryptocurrency is money that lives on the internet and isn't controlled by any single bank, company, or government.
- [What Is DeFi? Finance Without the Middleman, Explained](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-defi): DeFi rebuilds financial services — trading, lending, earning interest — as smart contracts with no company in the middle. How it works, and where the 'yield' really comes from.
- [What Is Ethereum? Smart Contracts, Explained Simply](https://ozgnos.com/library/what-is-ethereum): Bitcoin made money digital. Ethereum made agreements digital — programs that run on a blockchain with no company behind them. Here's the second big idea in crypto.
- [Why Does Crypto Have Value?](https://ozgnos.com/library/why-crypto-has-value): Crypto has value for the same reason most things do — people want it and there's a limited amount — but it's worth being honest that this is also the most debated question in crypto.
- [Why "DYOR" Matters (and How to Actually Do It)](https://ozgnos.com/library/why-dyor-matters): DYOR means "Do Your Own Research," and it's the habit of verifying things yourself instead of trusting anyone's word, including ours.
- [Why Leverage Destroys Beginners](https://ozgnos.com/library/why-leverage-destroys-beginners): Leverage is borrowed money that multiplies your wins and your losses — and in crypto's volatility, it usually multiplies the losses. The math, the trap, and how to say no.

## Tools & pages

- [Markets dashboard](https://ozgnos.com/markets): Live prices, market cap, and an educational indicator dashboard (RSI, moving averages, Fear & Greed).
- [Glossary](https://ozgnos.com/glossary): Plain-English definitions of crypto terms.
- [Newsletter](https://ozgnos.com/newsletter): Free email breakdowns.
- [About](https://ozgnos.com/about)
- [How we make money](https://ozgnos.com/disclosure): Full transparency on referral links.

## Citing Ozgnos

Content may be quoted with attribution and a link to the lesson. All lessons are in English, beginner level, and free to access.
